g.title: Disassembling the Side B Solar Panel Assembly
g.desc: This exploded view illustrates the disassembly of a layered assembly. The components include a tall wooden front panel with a large rectangular window cutout, a rectangular solar panel with a tactile grid pattern and small pins at the top, a wooden support plate with multiple vertical slots and holes, and a smaller rectangular wooden piece with tabs. The assembly is held together by four brass screws and four hex nuts. The screws are removed from the front of the windowed panel, passing through the support plate to the hex nuts on the back. Disassembly involves unscrewing these four fasteners to separate the wooden plates and free the solar panel sandwiched between them.

g.title: Piper Make Light Show Inventory List
g.desc: This inventory list for the Light Show kit includes several components. There are electronic parts: one square LED Matrix with a tactile grid of bulbs and a bundle of flexible Jumper Wires. The wooden parts are 5mm thick plywood and consist of one Front Panel, one Back Panel, and one Kickstand. The hardware includes four long 5/8-inch brass screws and four small hexagonal brass nuts. Two strips of squishy, adhesive-backed Foam Tape are also provided. Identify the wood by its flat, smooth surface and the hardware by its small, cold metal feel.

g.title: Disconnecting the 5-Wire Jumper Cable
g.desc: This disassembly step shows how to remove a jumper wire connector. Locate the small, black rectangular plastic block that has five colored wires protruding from its top. This connector is plugged into a row of five thin metal header pins mounted on a plywood piece. To disassemble, firmly grasp the black plastic connector and pull it straight up and away from the metal pins, following the direction of the orange dashed arrow. After removal, place the jumper wire assembly on its corresponding spot on the Component Sheet for organization.

text.tspan: Place each item on its spot

g.title: Step 2: Remove Screws from Front Panel
g.desc: Identify the assembly made of several stacked 5mm plywood pieces. Locate three #4-40 brass screws that pass through the front panel. These are secured by three hexagonal nuts on the back side. The screws are arranged vertically: one near the top, one in the center, and one near the bottom. To disassemble, unscrew each of the three screws and pull them out from the front side (left) while removing the matching hex nuts from the back side (right).

g.title: Step 3: Detaching the Kickstand
g.desc: In this disassembly step, you will separate the kickstand from the larger main body piece. The main body is a large, flat plywood plate with two vertical rectangular cutouts and a small hole in the center. The kickstand is a smaller rectangular piece of plywood with notches on one edge and a small T-shaped slot on its flat face. To disassemble, first unscrew and remove the #4-40 brass machine screw from the front of the main body's central hole. Then, remove the brass hex nut from the T-slot on the kickstand piece and pull the kickstand away from the back of the main body.

g.title: Disassembly Complete: Organizing Components on Sheet
g.desc: This final instructional step confirms that disassembly is finished. It instructs you to take all the individual laser-cut plywood pieces and brass hardware and place each one into its corresponding recessed or outlined spot on your tactile Component Sheet to ensure all parts are organized and accounted for.
